warmup_exercises.notes: Warmup — not counted in working volume

exercises.notes: **+2 kg vs 4/22** (was 12 kg × 15/15/20). Clean across all 3 sets. **Jacob:** 8 kg × 15
**+2 kg vs 4/22** (was 16 kg × 15/14/14). Cleaned up rep consistency. **Jacob:** 12 kg × 10 × 3
Held vs 4/22. Standing, rope to waist. **Jacob:** same load
Held vs 4/22. Face-away-from-stack, split-stance, rope overhead. **Jacob:** same load

session_notes:
Fasted training (6am, eating window starts at noon).
**Chest progression — both presses cleaned and bumped:** Incline DB 12 → 14 kg with 15/15/15 across (vs 4/22's 15/15/20 — the long set 3 last week predicted the load bump was earned). Flat DB 16 → 18 kg with 15/15/15 across (vs 4/22's 15/14/14 — rep consistency reclaimed at the higher load). Per [[project_pt_programming_style|PT programming style]], heavy compounds front-loaded and the numbers are landing clean.
**Triceps held flat:** Rope pushdown at 20 kg × 15 × 3 for the 3rd session in a row. Overhead extension at 29 kg × 15 × 3 for the 2nd session. Both ready for a load bump next push — Jacob matching on both says the loads are well-chosen but not stretching.
**Core held flat at 4/22 baseline:** V-up hybrid 15 × 2, plank 60s × 2. Light volume; could absorb a third set or add weighted variants.
**Shoulder gap — parked until Phase 2.** Four straight upper-body sessions with zero direct shoulder/rear-delt work (push 4/22, 4/29; pull 4/21, 4/28). Decision: not raising with PT — two sessions left, not worth the friction. Phase 2 programming (starting May 11) will rebuild direct shoulder volume from session one: press + lateral raise on push days, face pulls / rear delts on pull days.
Jacob still climbing on chest: 6 kg gap on incline (8 vs 14), 6 kg gap on flat (12 vs 18). Triceps and core fully matched.
